rationale: The citation is used for a specific analytical function, not just background listing. In Study 7, the authors explicitly report using a Johnson-Neyman floodlight analysis and cite Spiller et al. (2013) as the source for that probing approach in moderated regression. They then use that procedure to determine the PLRM threshold where the interaction effect becomes significant, so the cited paper directly supports result interpretation.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Spiller et al. (2013)'s moderated-regression probing guidance (especially floodlight/Johnson-Neyman logic) to analyze and interpret its interaction effects. It uses this method to locate the moderator range where subgoal success significantly changes persistence.
